service.location: Beach Church at the Boardwalk Pavilion

clips.title: What Zacchaeus knew about being seen
clips.label: 45-sec clip · share-ready

beginning.title: Curious about Jesus
beginning.body: Questions welcome — doubts too. Alpha is a relaxed dinner-table conversation about life and faith. No background required, nothing assumed.

returning.title: Coming back to faith
returning.body: Been away a while? You're not behind, and nobody's keeping score. Beach Church is the easiest way back in — sand, sea air, real people.

deepening.title: Ready to go deeper
deepening.body: Grow with people who get it — small groups, serving teams, and rhythms of prayer rooted in this place between the ocean and the town.

connect.devotionalDescription: Short daily readings for the walk, the commute, or the coffee. Yours when you connect.

invite.smsBody: Hey — come to Beach Church with me Sunday? 9 AM at the Ocean Grove boardwalk pavilion. I'll save you a seat. oceangrove.church

expect.title: When & where
expect.body: Sundays at 9:00 AM — Beach Church at the Boardwalk Pavilion in Ocean Grove. Off-season, we gather at 80 Embury Avenue.

expect.title: Come as you are
expect.body: Flip-flops, shorts, sand on your feet — we're a beach church. Dress for comfort, not a dress code.

expect.title: What worship's like
expect.body: A blend of hymns and modern songs, a message rooted in Scripture, communion open to everyone. About an hour, start to finish.

expect.title: Kids & families
expect.body: Kids are welcome in worship — always. We love the sound of children in church. Quiet activities and coloring pages available.

faq.question: Do I have to believe anything to come?
faq.answer: No. Come curious, come skeptical, come searching. You're welcome in the room while you're figuring it out.

faq.question: What if I haven't been to church in years?
faq.answer: Then you're in good company — a lot of us started (or re-started) right where you are. Nobody's keeping score.

faq.question: Is there coffee?
faq.answer: Yes. And it's good. Fellowship time before and after every service.

faq.question: Where do I park?
faq.answer: Street parking fills early on summer Sundays — arrive by 8:40 for the easiest spots, or better yet, bike or walk the boardwalk like half the congregation does.

weekly.title: Worship
weekly.body: Worship reminds us it's not about me — it connects us to God and to each other, and sends us into the world together with Jesus at the center.

weekly.title: Small Groups
weekly.body: Faith was never meant to be solo. Share life, scripture, and honest conversation with a handful of people who'll know your name.

weekly.title: Read the Bible in a Year
weekly.body: A simple plan that surveys the whole of Scripture and builds the habit of daily reading. Start January 1 — or today.

seasonal.title: Alpha
seasonal.body: A place to ask life's biggest questions — Does God exist? Who am I? What's my purpose? Each session explores a question of faith over food and honest conversation.

seasonal.title: This We Believe
seasonal.body: The core of Wesleyan faith and practice — studying the Bible as the testimony of God's never-ending grace, and living every day as a child of God.

seasonal.title: Financial Peace University
seasonal.body: Common-sense money principles with small-group accountability. Millions have used FPU to change their financial behavior for good.

milestones.title: Baptism
milestones.body: An outward sign of an inward change — a holy moment marking that you've become a new creature in Christ.

milestones.title: Membership
milestones.body: Like the Acts 2 church — devoted to teaching, fellowship, breaking bread, and prayer. More than joining: growing in love, truth, and accountability as one body.

milestones.title: Serve
milestones.body: Growth that stays inside never matures. Find a team — from Helping Hands to missions — and let your faith do something.
